description_long: This is a large outdoor umbrella with a wide, circular canopy featuring alternating beige and white concentric stripes. The canopy is supported by a central, straight pole that appears to be made of wood or a wood-like material. The fabric of the canopy is tightly stretched and segmented, giving it a structured, geometric appearance. The umbrella's tip is blunt and capped, providing a safe and finished look. The handle is straight and simple, with no visible ergonomic or decorative features. This type of umbrella is commonly used in urban parks, patios, or sidewalk cafes to provide shade and shelter from the sun.
description: A large, circular outdoor umbrella with beige and white concentric stripes and a straight wooden pole. The canopy is segmented and the tip is blunt and capped.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the full circular canopy with beige and white concentric stripes and the central pole.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated top view, still highlighting the striped canopy and central pole, with minimal visible difference from other views.
description_view_2: Another rotated top view, showing the same striped pattern and pole, with no significant new features.
description_view_3: Final rotated top view, maintaining the visibility of the striped canopy and central pole, similar to previous views.
